metadata.project_name: Crypto Analytics and ML Signal Platform
metadata.purpose: Static visual guide for recruiters and reviewers.
scope_notes: This is an analytics and ML signal platform, not a trading bot.
The page is documentation-only and does not run ingestion, training, deployment, or cloud writes.
MLflow, Optuna, and MLflow Registry are optional and off by default.
subset9 and microstructure features are research/manual candidates, not production defaults.

coverage.summary: Reliable 5-year backfill coverage is currently strongest for Binance trades, ETF indicators, macro indicators, and funding data. Other sources are partial, experimental, or not fully live-ready.

tabs.label: Overview
tabs.title: End-to-end crypto analytics and conservative ML signal platform
tabs.summary: The project demonstrates batch ingestion, experimental streaming, BigQuery/dbt modeling, Kestra orchestration, Terraform-managed cloud infrastructure, Dockerized runtimes, monitoring marts, and conservative ML signal workflows.

cards.title: What reviewers should notice
cards.body: The repository ties together data engineering, analytics modeling, orchestration, MLOps controls, and CI/CD gates while documenting source-coverage limits explicitly.

cards.title: Production boundary
cards.body: The default ML contract remains conservative. Research scripts and feature candidates are separated from production entrypoints and are not auto-promoted.

cards.title: Current data confidence
cards.body: The strongest historical coverage is Binance trades, ETF, macro, and funding. Other feeds can be useful analytically but should be treated as partial or experimental.

important_files.label: Portfolio overview
important_files.note: Top-level project summary and safety notes.

important_files.label: Architecture guide
important_files.note: End-to-end platform architecture and operational boundaries.

warnings: Do not present the system as automated trading infrastructure.
Do not infer model edge from partial source coverage.

tabs.label: Batch
tabs.title: Batch collectors, backfills, validation, and cloud landing tables
tabs.summary: Batch ingestion is the trusted historical path for this project. It supports collectors, selected 5-year backfills, quality checks, monitoring, and downstream dbt/ML preparation.

cards.title: Strongest sources
cards.body: Binance trades, ETF indicators, macro indicators, and funding data are the most reliable 5-year backfill set.

cards.title: Partial sources
cards.body: Options, liquidation, stablecoin, exchange reserve, and sentiment feeds are useful but source-dependent and not uniformly live-ready.

cards.title: Operational caution
cards.body: Backfills can read and write large volumes, so they should only run with intentional cloud targets and cost expectations.

important_files.label: Backfill scripts
important_files.note: Historical loaders for selected trusted sources.

warnings: Do not run backfills casually.
Avoid documenting or linking local .env files, service account keys, local output data, or Terraform state.

tabs.label: Streaming
tabs.title: Experimental streaming producers, Flink logic, and BigQuery sink preparation
tabs.summary: Streaming supports freshness experiments and future prediction-input automation, but it is not the primary trusted full-history path yet.

cards.title: Best use
cards.body: Recent market/context freshness and future automation once monitoring proves completeness and sink stability.

cards.title: Current posture
cards.body: Treat as partial compared with the batch path. It should not be the sole source for historical training coverage.

warnings: Validate topic, connector, sink, and freshness health before tying streaming output to automatic prediction.
Do not expose local connector credentials or key files in documentation.

tabs.label: dbt
tabs.title: Layered BigQuery models for analytics, dashboards, ML, and monitoring
tabs.summary: The dbt project normalizes raw sources, aligns time-series features, builds reusable facts/dimensions, exposes dashboard marts, prepares ML tables, and monitors health.

cards.title: Reviewer lens
cards.body: The model naming convention makes the data lineage legible: stg for source normalization, int for aligned features, marts for downstream contracts.

cards.title: ML contract boundary
cards.body: dbt may expose additive research columns, but production training only uses the conservative ml/feature_list.yml contract.

important_files.label: ML marts
important_files.note: Training data, prediction input, labels, metrics, and quality tables.

warnings: Avoid label leakage when changing ML marts.
Inspect partitioning, clustering, and materialization before cloud-backed builds.

tabs.label: ML/MLOps
tabs.title: Artifact-first training, prediction, optional tracking, and promotion gates
tabs.summary: The ML subsystem trains and predicts from dbt ML marts while preserving feature-contract consistency, local-first research, optional MLflow/Optuna/Registry support, and conservative promotion checks.

cards.title: Default production posture
cards.body: Prediction loads artifacts by default. Registry loading is optional and only used when explicitly configured.

cards.title: Research separation
cards.body: local_*.py scripts support diagnostics, AutoML, ablation, keeper validation, and microstructure experiments without becoming production serving paths.

cards.title: subset9 / microstructure
cards.body: These features are research/manual candidates and have not replaced the baseline production contract.

important_files.label: Training entrypoint
important_files.note: Trains models and writes artifacts when configured.

important_files.label: Prediction entrypoint
important_files.note: Loads latest artifact or optional registry model.

important_files.label: Production feature contract
important_files.note: Conservative default contract; do not change casually.

important_files.label: Promotion gate
important_files.note: Prevents automatic promotion of worse candidates.

warnings: MLflow, Optuna, and Registry are optional/off by default.
Do not modify ml/feature_list.yml as part of documentation polish.
Do not run training during documentation-only review.

tabs.label: Kestra
tabs.title: GKE-oriented flows for ingestion, dbt, ML, monitoring, quality, and PR previews
tabs.summary: Kestra represents the production-style orchestration layer. Flow groups are separated by purpose, with ML deployment gated separately from batch/dbt deployment.

cards.title: Flow grouping
cards.body: GKE flows live under kestra/flows-gke and are grouped into raw, dbt, ML, streaming, monitoring, quality, preview, and master directories.

cards.title: ML deploy gate
cards.body: ENABLE_ML_KESTRA_DEPLOY controls ML flow deployment. Batch and dbt flow deployment stay independent of that flag.

important_files.label: Raw flows
important_files.note: Batch ingestion snapshots and intraday refresh.

important_files.label: dbt flows
important_files.note: Hourly, intraday, daily market, macro, and ETF transforms.

important_files.label: ML flows
important_files.note: Training, prediction, and strategy matrix flows gated separately.

warnings: Do not enable ML deploy simply to make checks green.
Do not add triggers to PR preview flows.

tabs.label: CI/CD
tabs.title: GitHub Actions quality checks, Docker gating, Kestra deploy planning, and PR safety
tabs.summary: CI/CD keeps documentation, code quality, Docker builds, and Kestra deployment safer by running targeted checks and skipping expensive work when no deployable runtime changes exist.

cards.title: Cost-aware validation
cards.body: Docker builds and Kestra deploy steps are gated so docs-only or non-runtime PRs avoid unnecessary cloud-adjacent work.

cards.title: Required checks
cards.body: The required-gate workflow aggregates expected checks so skipped deploy paths do not incorrectly block safe PRs.

important_files.label: Quality checks
important_files.note: Tests, repo guard, and static validation.

important_files.label: Docker build/push
important_files.note: Gated image build and smoke workflow.

important_files.label: Kestra deploy
important_files.note: Deploys GKE flows when plan allows it.

warnings: Docs-only changes should not require training, backfills, deploys, or cloud writes.
Secrets, local artifacts, and Terraform state must stay out of docs and commits.

tabs.label: Repo Map
tabs.title: Repository areas grouped by reviewer intent
tabs.summary: Use this map to quickly find ingestion, streaming, dbt, orchestration, ML, infrastructure, CI/CD, Docker, tests, and documentation areas.

cards.title: Fast review path
cards.body: Start with README.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/repository_map.md, then use this explorer to jump into the subsystem files.

cards.title: Sensitive files omitted
cards.body: Local env files, key files, Terraform state, logs, generated outputs, and local artifacts are intentionally excluded from this explorer.

warnings: Avoid linking secret-like files or local generated artifacts.
Treat Terraform state and tfvars as sensitive operational files.

tabs.label: Research vs Production
tabs.title: Production defaults are conservative; research candidates remain manual
tabs.summary: The project keeps production entrypoints, feature contracts, and deployment gates separate from local research scripts, experimental features, and optional MLOps integrations.

cards.title: Production default files
cards.body: ml/train_model.py, ml/predict_latest.py, ml/feature_list.yml, ml/model_loader.py, and ml/promotion_gate.py define the conservative production workflow.

cards.title: Research-only files
cards.body: ml/local_*.py and research config candidates support local exploration, diagnostics, and model readiness review. They are not production serving paths.

cards.title: MLOps optionality
cards.body: MLflow logging, Optuna tuning, and MLflow Registry integration require explicit configuration and are off by default.

important_files.label: Production feature list
important_files.note: Not changed by this documentation work.

important_files.label: Optional registry
important_files.note: Alias-based registry integration, not required for prediction.

warnings: subset9 and microstructure features are research/manual candidates, not production defaults.
This project should not be described as a trading bot.
Do not run training or promotion as part of docs-only review.

tabs.label: AI codebase map
tabs.title: Optional AI-assisted exploration placeholder
tabs.summary: Understand-Anything can be used later as an interactive codebase exploration layer, but it is not required to run, review, or understand this project.

cards.title: What it is
cards.body: Understand-Anything is optional and is not part of the project runtime, pipeline, training workflow, or deployment process.

cards.title: How to generate later
cards.body: Run /understand --language en, then /understand-dashboard in an environment where Understand-Anything is available.

cards.title: Current state
cards.body: This repo may not include .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json. If generated later, it can become an additional exploration layer.

cards.title: Primary docs remain
cards.body: README.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/repository_map.md, and this static explorer remain the primary documentation.

warnings: No parser for .understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json is implemented in this phase.
Do not require Understand-Anything for normal project operation.
